response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack software developer with over 14 years of experience across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but his background has virtually no overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, GPU/AI accelerator hardware architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep-learning workload optimization at the hardware level — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest relevant experience is software-level latency optimization using async I/O in DLang, which operates several abstraction layers above the silicon-level work this role demands.
strengths: 14+ years of broad engineering experience with demonstrated performance-aware development (low-latency, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Recent involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, showing willingness to engage with AI-adjacent domains
Strong polyglot engineering background with systems-level exposure (C/C++, DLang, embedded, robotic arms) indicating adaptability
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from his background
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator expertise; all preferred qualifications are unmet
No deep learning or neural network workload knowledge; his AI exposure is limited to software-layer agentic tooling, not hardware optimization

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture background
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator experience
